description: city in Twin Falls County, Idaho, United States
description_wiki: Buhl is a city located on the old Oregon Trail in the western half of Twin Falls County, Idaho, United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Tennessee ( , locally ), officially the State of Tennessee, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: city in and state capital of Idaho, United States
description_wiki: Boise (locally   also ) is the capital and most populous city in the U.S. state of Idaho.

description: high school in Boise, Ada County, Idaho
description_wiki:
Boise High School is a public secondary school in Boise, Idaho, one of five
traditional high schools within the city limits, four of which are in the Boise School District.

description: public research university in Boise, Idaho, USA
description_wiki: Boise State University (BSU) is a public research university in Boise, Idaho, United States.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: governmental program conceived by the Civil Aeronautics Authority of the United States
description_wiki: The Civilian Pilot Training Program (CPTP) was a flight training program (1938–1944) sponsored by the United States government with the stated purpose of increasing the number of civilian pilots, though having a clear impact on military preparedness.

description: former movie theater in Boise, Idaho, United States
light aircraft family
description_wiki: The Piper J-3 Cub is an American light aircraft that was built between 1938 and 1947 by Piper Aircraft.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: Roman Catholic college
description_wiki: Saint Mary's College of California is a private Catholic college in Moraga, California, United States.

description: city in Orange County, California, United States
description_wiki: Los Alamitos (lohs AL-ə-MEE-tohs; Spanish for 'the little cottonwoods') is a city in Orange County, California, which was incorporated in March 1960.

description: American entertainer (1903–2003)
description_wiki: Lester Townes "Bob" Hope (né Leslie Townes Hope; May 29, 1903 – July 27, 2003) was a British-born American comedian, actor, entertainer and producer with a career that spanned nearly 80 years and achievements in vaudeville, network radio, television, and USO Tours.

description: American bandleader, actor (1905-1985)
description_wiki: James Kern Kyser (June 18, 1905 – July 23, 1985), known as Kay Kyser, was an American bandleader and radio personality of the 1930s and 1940s.

aliases: Mountain Home Air Force Base, ID
description: United States Air Force base near Mountain Home, Idaho, United States
description_wiki: Mountain Home Air Force Base (IATA: MUO, ICAO: KMUO, FAA LID: MUO) is a United States Air Force (USAF) installation in the western United States.

description: civil/military airport in Boise, Idaho, U.S.
city in and county seat of Bee County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Beeville is a city in Bee County, Texas, United States.

description: seat of Kleberg County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Kingsville is a city in the southern region of the U.S. state of Texas and the county seat of Kleberg County.

description: city and county seat of Nueces County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Corpus Christi ( KOR-pəs KRIS-tee; Latin for 'Body of Christ') is a coastal city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: 1935 trainer aircraft family by North American Aviation
description_wiki: The North American Aviation T-6 Texan is an American single-engined advanced trainer aircraft, which was used to train pilots of the United States Army Air Forces (USAAF), United States Air Force (USAF), United States Navy, Royal Air Force, Royal Canadian Air Force and other air forces of the British Commonwealth during World War II and into the 1970s.

description: trainer aircraft family by Ryan
description_wiki: The Ryan STs are a series of two seat, low-wing monoplane aircraft built in the United States by the Ryan Aeronautical Company.

description: 1937 fighter aircraft family by the Grumman Corporation
description_wiki: The Grumman F4F Wildcat is an American carrier-based fighter aircraft that entered service in 1940 with the United States Navy and the British Royal Navy.

description: one of the Great Lakes of North America
description_wiki: Lake Michigan (  MISH-ig-ən) is one of the five Great Lakes of North America.

entity: German submarine campaign of World War II
surface_forms: German subs off the coast of Florida
description: 1939 longest continuous military campaign in World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of the Atlantic, the longest-continuous military campaign in World War II, ran from 1939 to the defeat of Nazi Germany in 1945, covering a major part of the naval history of World War II. At its core was the Allied naval blockade of Germany, announced the day after the declaration of war, and Germany's subsequent counterblockade.
